userStories.title: Schema, DTO, and i18n foundations for Graphify
userStories.description: Add project-level Graphify flags to persistence and DTOs, extend RAG document source typing to include code documents, and add localized disabled-feature messaging. This story establishes the type and contract baseline required by service, endpoint, and CLI stories.
acceptanceCriteria: Given a project is created without a graphifyEnabled value, when it is persisted, then Project.graphifyEnabled is false by default.
When AddDocumentDto receives source='code', then validation accepts the payload.
When AddDocumentDto receives source outside ['ticket', 'doc', 'manual', 'code'], then request validation returns 400.
When IndexDocumentInput.source is used, then 'code' is an accepted source literal.
When ProjectResponseDto.from(project) is called, then dto.graphifyEnabled equals project.graphifyEnabled.
When ProjectResponseDto.from(project) is called, then dto.graphifyLastImportedAt equals project.graphifyLastImportedAt as Date or null.
When UpdateProjectDto receives graphifyEnabled=true, then validation accepts the payload.
When i18n keys are loaded, then rag.graphifyDisabled exists with a non-empty value in both en/rag.json and zh/rag.json.
suggestedCriteria: When ProjectResponseDto.from(project) is called for legacy records with null graphifyLastImportedAt, then dto.graphifyLastImportedAt is null rather than undefined.
When UpdateProjectDto omits graphifyEnabled, then existing update behavior for other fields remains unchanged.

userStories.title: Implement RagService Graphify import and source-type purge
userStories.description: Extend RagService with `deleteAllBySourceType()` and `importGraphify()` to support idempotent code-node ingestion from Graphify payloads. Reuse existing index pipeline and embed content generation from node/link adjacency.
acceptanceCriteria: When RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') is called, then it deletes all records with source='code' for that project and returns the deleted row count.
When RagService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') is called and no code records exist, then it returns 0.
When RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) is called, then it calls deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code') before indexing nodes.
When RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, []) is called, then each indexed node content is formatted as '{type} {label} in {source_file}' when source_file is present.
When RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) is called, then node content includes '{relation} {neighbor_label}' for links where the node is the source.
When RagService.importGraphify(projectId, nodes, links) indexes nodes, then indexDocument() receives source='code', sourceId=node.id, and metadata containing label, type, source_file, and community.
When RagService.importGraphify(projectId, [], []) is called, then it returns { imported: 0, cleared: N } where N is the count returned by deleteAllBySourceType.
suggestedCriteria: When a node.type is absent, then imported metadata.type is 'node' and content starts with 'node {label}'.
When a node has no source_file, then generated content omits the 'in {source_file}' segment.
When links reference an unknown node id, then importGraphify still indexes known nodes without throwing.

userStories.title: Expose ADMIN Graphify import endpoint
userStories.description: Add `POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify` with DTO validation, ADMIN authorization, project/feature checks, RagService import wiring, timestamp update, and e2e coverage for happy and failure paths.
acceptanceCriteria: Given ADMIN auth and a valid body, when POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify is called, then the response is { ret: 0, data: { imported: number, cleared: number } }.
When a non-ADMIN caller sends POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify, then the response status is 403.
When POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify targets a missing project slug, then the response status is 404.
When POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify is called for a project with graphifyEnabled=false, then the response status is 400.
Given nodes=[], when POST /projects/:slug/kb/import/graphify succeeds, then response data is { imported: 0, cleared: 0 } and no node indexing is invoked.
When ImportGraphifyDto receives a body missing nodes, then validation returns 400.
When ImportGraphifyDto receives nodes with links omitted, then validation accepts the body.
When the import route executes, then it calls ragService.importGraphify(project.id, dto.nodes, dto.links ?? []) and returns that result.
Given a successful import, when controller processing completes, then project.graphifyLastImportedAt is updated to the current UTC timestamp.
Given a successful import, when GET /projects/:slug is called next, then the returned project includes graphifyLastImportedAt.
suggestedCriteria: When links is provided as an empty array, then response shape remains { ret: 0, data: { imported, cleared } } with no schema coercion differences.
When graphify import is rejected due to graphifyEnabled=false, then no deletion or indexing side effects occur.

userStories.title: Enforce Graphify disable cleanup in ProjectsService
userStories.description: Wire RagService into ProjectsService update flow so that toggling graphifyEnabled from true to false purges code-source documents, with warn-level non-blocking failure behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Given current graphifyEnabled=true, when ProjectsService.update() receives graphifyEnabled=false, then it calls ragService.deleteAllBySourceType(projectId, 'code').
When ProjectsService.update() is called without graphifyEnabled in the payload, then it does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType.
Given current graphifyEnabled=false, when ProjectsService.update() receives graphifyEnabled=true, then it does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType.
Given current graphifyEnabled=true, when ProjectsService.update() receives graphifyEnabled=true, then it does not call ragService.deleteAllBySourceType.
When ragService.deleteAllBySourceType throws during toggle-off cleanup, then ProjectsService.update() logs at warn level and does not re-throw.
When ProjectsModule is initialized, then RagModule is imported so RagService is available for ProjectsService dependency injection.
suggestedCriteria: When cleanup fails during toggle-off, then the persisted project record still has graphifyEnabled=false.
When toggle-off cleanup succeeds, then update response payload remains consistent with existing ProjectsService.update() contract.

userStories.title: Add CLI Graphify import command
userStories.description: Implement `koda kb import --project <slug> --graphify <path> [--json]` in CLI by resolving auth, reading/parsing graph.json, extracting nodes/links, invoking generated API endpoint, and honoring defined exit codes and output formats.
acceptanceCriteria: When `koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json` succeeds, then process exits 0 and stdout prints `✓ Graphify import complete: {imported} code nodes indexed ({cleared} cleared)`.
When `koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./graph.json --json` succeeds, then process exits 0 and stdout prints raw JSON response.
When `koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./missing.json` is run, then process exits 1 and stderr prints a file-not-found error.
When `koda kb import --project koda --graphify ./invalid.json` is run with non-JSON file content, then process exits 1 and stderr prints a parse-error message.
When `koda kb import` is run without --project, then process exits 3.
When `koda kb import` is run without --graphify, then process exits 3.
Given auth configuration is absent, when `koda kb import` runs, then process exits 2 and does not call the API.
When a graphify file contains additional top-level keys, then the API request body includes only `{ nodes, links }` extracted from the parsed file.
suggestedCriteria: When links is absent in graph.json, then command sends links as undefined or empty according to generated client contract without including unrelated keys.
When API returns a validation error, then command routes the error through handleApiError(err) and preserves configured exit behavior.

analysis: This is an enhancement to an existing monorepo with API and CLI work in scope, plus a Prisma schema migration. The provided context indicates apps/api (Fastify + Jest + Prisma) and apps/cli (Jest), and the spec gives explicit target files and dependency order. Implementation should follow existing patterns in API DTOs/controllers/services, existing RAG indexing flow, project update flow, and CLI command conventions. Primary API impact areas are Prisma Project fields (`graphifyEnabled`, `graphifyLastImportedAt`), RAG source typing (`code`), new graph import DTOs and endpoint, and RagService ingestion logic that transforms Graphify node/link payloads into embeddable content and indexes via existing `indexDocument()`. Project update logic must enforce cleanup when toggling graphify off, with warn-level logging and non-blocking behavior on cleanup failure. CLI impact area is a new `koda kb import --graphify` sub-command that reads local JSON, extracts only `nodes`/`links`, calls generated API client, and follows existing exit code and error handling patterns. Existing dependencies likely include generated OpenAPI client calls in CLI and Nest guards/role checks in API; route should reuse ADMIN authorization pattern already present in rag controller. Test impact spans API unit tests (`rag.service.spec.ts`, `projects.service.spec.ts`), API e2e endpoint tests, and CLI command tests. Key risks: (1) regression in `source` enum/validation affecting existing document ingestion, (2) adjacency-to-content generation differences versus expected text format, especially handling missing `type`, `source_file`, and empty links, (3) accidental behavior change in project update transaction semantics when adding cleanup call, (4) mismatch between endpoint error codes and current not-found/validation exception conventions, and (5) CLI generated client method naming drift. Backward compatibility is mostly additive; existing clients remain valid, with opt-in gate defaulting false. Migration path: apply Prisma migration adding new project fields with safe defaults/nullability; deploy API changes; regenerate client as needed; update CLI command. Operationally, projects stay unaffected until `graphifyEnabled` is set true. Re-import is idempotent by full clear of `source:'code'` before index. Search path remains unchanged because existing RRF already mixes sources; once code docs are indexed, they naturally appear in KB search results.
